description_long: This is a small rural farmstead composed of two main timber-framed buildings with steep, thatched roofs and a long, low outbuilding connected by wooden fencing. The larger building features visible crossbeams, a central entrance, and several barrels and crates near the front, suggesting storage or utility use. The smaller building, set apart by the fence, has a similar timber and thatch construction, likely serving as a barn or livestock shelter. The connecting fence forms a partial enclosure, creating a courtyard or working area between the structures. The outbuilding along one side appears to be a stable or storage shed, with a simple, elongated form and a matching thatched roof. The overall appearance is rustic and functional, typical of medieval or early modern European farm architecture.
description: A rustic farmstead with timber-framed, thatched-roof buildings and wooden fencing forming a courtyard. The structures include a main house, a barn, and a long outbuilding.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the farmstead from above, highlighting the main house, barn, and the fenced courtyard between them.
description_view_1: View 1 presents the side of the long outbuilding and the rear of the main house, emphasizing the length of the structure.
description_view_2: View 2 displays the back of the main house and barn, with the fence connecting the two buildings clearly visible.
description_view_3: View 3 offers a direct look at the front of the main house and the barn, showing entryways and architectural details.
